mitre_attack: AML.T0024 - Exfiltration via ML Inference API
cwe: CWE-200 - Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
CWE-359 - Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor
CWE-312 - Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information

threat.title: AI/ML Training Data Extraction Attack - LLM Memorization and Model Inversion
threat.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) and other ML models can memorize and regurgitate verbatim training data including PII, credentials, proprietary code, and other sensitive information. Attackers exploit this through prompt engineering, repeated token attacks, membership inference, model inversion, and gradient attacks to extract data that was never intended to be exposed. This affects foundation models (GPT, LLaMA), fine-tuned models, code generation systems (Copilot), and image diffusion models.

attack.scenario:
1. Reconnaissance: Attacker identifies target LLM service (ChatGPT API, internal fine-tuned model, or code generation tool)
2. Baseline Probing: Sends normal queries to understand model behavior and identify potential memorization patterns
3. Repeated Token Injection: Crafts prompts with repeated tokens (e.g., 'poem poem poem...') to force model divergence from safety alignment
4. Targeted Extraction: Uses carefully crafted prompts to elicit specific types of data (email addresses, phone numbers, API keys, code snippets)
5. PII Harvesting: Extracts memorized personal information including names, addresses, social security numbers, medical records from training data
6. Credential Exfiltration: Retrieves hardcoded credentials, API keys, database connection strings, and private keys that were present in training datasets
prerequisites: Model trained on datasets containing sensitive information (PII, credentials, proprietary data)
API access to the model (public or internal endpoint)
No differential privacy guarantees during training (epsilon > 1.0)
Insufficient output filtering or content safety mechanisms
High memorization rate (common in models with large capacity trained on small, repeated datasets)

cve_references.description: ChatGPT Training Data Extraction via Repeated Token Attack (Nasr et al., 2023)
cve_references.note: No CVE assigned; documented in arXiv:2311.17035 and confirmed by OpenAI

real_world_breaches.impact: Researchers extracted over 10,000 unique memorized training examples for $200 in API costs, including PII, URLs, complete poems, Bitcoin addresses, and copyrighted scientific research
real_world_breaches.root_cause: GPT-3.5-turbo memorized verbatim training data; repeated token prompts caused divergence from alignment fine-tuning

real_world_breaches.incident: GitHub Copilot Secret and Credential Leakage
real_world_breaches.impact: Researchers extracted 2,702 hardcoded credentials from Copilot (129 from CodeWhisperer) using 900 crafted prompts; exposed API keys, database passwords, and private repository code
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Codex model trained on public GitHub repositories containing exposed secrets; no adequate sanitization of training data

real_world_breaches.impact: Researchers extracted exact copies of training images from Stable Diffusion, including faces and copyrighted artwork; demonstrated 92% success rate in identity inference attacks
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Diffusion models memorize training images more than GANs; insufficient privacy-preserving training techniques

real_world_breaches.impact: After OpenAI patched single-token repetition filtering, attackers used multi-token sequences to continue extracting memorized data from GPT-3.5 and GPT-4
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Incomplete mitigation; filtering only applied to single repeated tokens, not multi-token patterns

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Training data extraction via repeated token attack (responsible disclosure by Google DeepMind researchers)
CodexLeaks: Privacy leaks from GitHub Copilot code generation

security_research.title: Scalable Extraction of Training Data from (Production) Language Models
security_research.key_finding: Extracted 10,000+ unique memorized examples from ChatGPT for $200; demonstrated that alignment fine-tuning is insufficient to prevent training data extraction

security_research.title: CodexLeaks: Privacy Leaks from Code Generation Language Models in GitHub Copilot
security_research.key_finding: Semi-automated pipeline for extracting PII and secrets from Codex; 2,702 credentials extracted from Copilot with crafted prompts

security_research.title: Extracting Training Data from Diffusion Models
security_research.key_finding: Diffusion models are significantly less private than GANs; extracted verbatim training images including faces and copyrighted content

security_research.title: Identity-Focused Inference and Extraction Attacks on Diffusion Models
security_research.key_finding: Achieved 89% membership inference success rate, 92% identity inference rate, and 91.6% data extraction accuracy on diffusion models

security_research.title: Membership Inference Attacks Against Fine-tuned Large Language Models via Self-prompt Calibration
security_research.key_finding: SPV-MIA attack raised membership inference AUC from 0.7 to 0.9 on LLMs using self-calibrated probabilistic variation

mitigations.title: Implement Differential Privacy with PyTorch Opacus
mitigations.description: Apply differential privacy during model training using DP-SGD (Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent) to mathematically limit memorization of individual training examples. This provides formal privacy guarantees with configurable epsilon values.

code_example.code:
from opacus import PrivacyEngine
from opacus.validators import ModuleValidator
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim

# Define your model (must be compatible with Opacus)
model = nn.Sequential(
    nn.Linear(784, 256),
    nn.ReLU(),
    nn.Linear(256, 10)
)

# Validate and fix model for differential privacy
model = ModuleValidator.fix(model)
model = model.to('cuda')

# Create optimizer
optimizer = optim.Adam(model.parameters(), lr=1e-3)

# Initialize Privacy Engine with strict privacy parameters
privacy_engine = PrivacyEngine()

model, optimizer, train_loader = privacy_engine.make_private_with_epsilon(
    module=model,
    optimizer=optimizer,
    data_loader=train_loader,
    epochs=10,
    target_epsilon=3.0,      # Lower = stronger privacy (1.0-10.0 range)
    target_delta=1e-5,       # Should be < 1/training_set_size
    max_grad_norm=1.0,       # Gradient clipping threshold
)

print(f"Using sigma={optimizer.noise_multiplier} and C={max_grad_norm}")

# Training loop with privacy tracking
for epoch in range(10):
    for batch_idx, (data, target) in enumerate(train_loader):
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        output = model(data)
        loss = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()(output, target)
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()

    # Track privacy budget at each epoch
    epsilon = privacy_engine.get_epsilon(delta=1e-5)
    print(f"Epoch {epoch}: Loss={loss.item():.4f}, ε={epsilon:.2f}")

    # Stop training if privacy budget exceeded
    if epsilon > 3.0:
        print("Privacy budget exhausted!")
        break

# Final privacy guarantee
final_epsilon = privacy_engine.get_epsilon(delta=1e-5)
print(f"Final privacy guarantee: (ε={final_epsilon:.2f}, δ=1e-5)")

iso27001_controls: A.18.1.4 - Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
nist_ai_rmf: GOVERN 1.2 - Accountability structures are in place
MAP 1.5 - Privacy and data protection risks are assessed
validation_steps: Verify epsilon value is < 10.0 for production (< 3.0 for high-sensitivity data)
Monitor privacy budget consumption during training

mitigations.title: Training Data Sanitization and PII Removal
mitigations.description: Scan and remove sensitive information from training datasets before model training using automated PII detection, regex patterns, and ML-based classifiers.

code_example.code:
from presidio_analyzer import AnalyzerEngine
from presidio_anonymizer import AnonymizerEngine
from presidio_anonymizer.entities import OperatorConfig

analyzer = AnalyzerEngine()
anonymizer = AnonymizerEngine()

PII_ENTITIES = [
    "CREDIT_CARD", "CRYPTO", "EMAIL_ADDRESS", "IP_ADDRESS",
    "PERSON", "PHONE_NUMBER", "US_SSN", "LOCATION"
]

def sanitize_text(text, threshold=0.6):
    results = analyzer.analyze(
        text=text,
        language='en',
        entities=PII_ENTITIES,
        score_threshold=threshold
    )

    anonymized = anonymizer.anonymize(
        text=text,
        analyzer_results=results,
        operators={
            "DEFAULT": OperatorConfig("replace", {"new_value": "<REDACTED>"}),
            "EMAIL_ADDRESS": OperatorConfig("replace", {"new_value": "user@example.com"}),
        }
    )

    return anonymized.text, results

nist_ai_rmf: MAP 1.5 - Privacy and data protection risks
validation_steps: Run sanitization on sample dataset and verify PII removal
Check sanitization report for coverage (should detect >95% of common PII)

mitigations.title: LLM Output Filtering and Content Safety
mitigations.description: Implement real-time output filtering to detect and block potential training data leakage in model responses.
iso27001_controls: A.18.1.4 - Privacy and protection of PII
nist_ai_rmf: MEASURE 2.7 - AI system output is reviewed
validation_steps: Test filter with sample PII (should block SSN, credit cards)

mitigations.title: Implement Rate Limiting and Anomaly Detection for Inference APIs
mitigations.description: Detect and block suspicious query patterns indicating training data extraction attempts.

code_example.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
import redis
from collections import Counter

app = FastAPI()
redis_client = redis.Redis(host='localhost', decode_responses=True)

MAX_REQUESTS_PER_MINUTE = 20
REPEATED_TOKEN_THRESHOLD = 10

def detect_repeated_tokens(text, threshold=10):
    tokens = text.split()
    if not tokens:
        return False
    token_counts = Counter(tokens)
    return max(token_counts.values()) >= threshold

@app.post("/v1/chat/completions")
async def chat_completion(data: dict):
    prompt = data.get('prompt', '')

    if detect_repeated_tokens(prompt, REPEATED_TOKEN_THRESHOLD):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=400,
            detail="Invalid prompt: excessive token repetition detected"
        )

    return {"response": "LLM response here"}

validation_steps: Test rate limiting with burst requests
Validate repeated token detection with known attack prompts

indicators: Unusually high API request rates from single client (>100/hour)
Prompts containing repeated tokens (e.g., 'poem poem poem...')
Requests with very low entropy or very high repetition
Prompts explicitly requesting verbatim recall or specific PII
Outputs containing PII, credentials, or API keys

log_queries.query:
// Detect anomalous LLM API inference patterns
let TimeWindow = 1h;
let SuspiciousThreshold = 100;
AzureDiagnostics
| where ResourceType == "MACHINELEARNINGSERVICES"
| where OperationName == "InferenceRequest"
| extend ClientIP = tostring(parse_json(properties_s).clientIpAddress)
| extend PromptLength = strlen(tostring(parse_json(properties_s).prompt))
| summarize
    RequestCount = count(),
    AvgPromptLength = avg(PromptLength),
    UniquePrompts = dcount(tostring(parse_json(properties_s).prompt))
    by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, TimeWindow)
| where RequestCount > SuspiciousThreshold
    or (RequestCount > 20 and UniquePrompts < 5)
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, RequestCount, UniquePrompts, ThreatLevel = case(
    RequestCount > 200, "Critical",
    RequestCount > 100, "High",
    "Medium"
)
| order by RequestCount desc

// Detect PII leakage in LLM responses
AzureDiagnostics
| where ResourceType == "MACHINELEARNINGSERVICES"
| extend Response = tostring(parse_json(properties_s).response)
| extend
    HasSSN = Response matches regex @"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b",
    HasEmail = Response matches regex @"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b",
    HasPhone = Response matches regex @"\b\d{3}[-.]?\d{3}[-.]?\d{4}\b"
| where HasSSN or HasEmail or HasPhone
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP = tostring(parse_json(properties_s).clientIpAddress), PIIType = case(
    HasSSN, "SSN",
    HasEmail, "Email",
    HasPhone, "Phone Number",
    "Unknown"
)

index=ml_api sourcetype=inference_logs
| where len(prompt) < 20 OR match(prompt, "(\w+\s+){5,}\1")
| stats count as request_count by client_ip, _time span=1h
| where request_count > 50
| table _time, client_ip, request_count

event.dataset: "ml.inference" AND event.category: "api"
| where prompt.length < 20 OR prompt.repetition_score > 10
| stats count() by source.ip, bucket(@timestamp, 1h)
| where count > 100

fields @timestamp, requestParameters.endpointName, sourceIPAddress
| filter eventName = "InvokeEndpoint"
| stats count() as request_count by sourceIPAddress, bin(1h)
| filter request_count > 100
| sort request_count desc
